page: Amazon Web Services m8id.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and is supported by a large 480 MB L3 cache. Memory configuration includes 128 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM operating at 7 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core.

This instance includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, offering high-speed local data access. It does not feature a GPU. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 15 Gbps. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate vector calculations and deep learning operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8id.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance profiles. It achieves top-tier results in single-core Geekbench tests, image processing, and text processing. Memory performance is robust, with large-block read-only bandwidth reaching 179.94 GB/sec. Additionally, the instance exhibits strong capabilities in LLM inference tasks, particularly for text generation with smaller models. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of high-speed local NVMe storage alongside DDR5 memory and dedicated compute resources provides a balanced resource density for workloads requiring both high-speed local scratch space and modern CPU instruction sets.

The resource profile of the m8id.8xlarge makes it highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, database hosting, static web serving, and software compilation. It is also well-suited for medium-scale data processing, text and image manipulation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Operating at 3.9 GHz, it provides 32 dedicated vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The architecture supports advanced instructions like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which enhance vector and matrix operations. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, image processing, and text processing, alongside strong memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression. With a baseline network speed of 15 Gbps and no GPU, this instance offers a balanced resource density. It is highly suited for database hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

128 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
